---
title: "Routing Heads and Specialist Cores"
chapter_id: "routing-heads-and-specialist-cores"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "octopus_router"
  - "rmi"
  - "beastbrain"
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "rgs"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Routing Heads and Specialist Cores

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `routing-heads-and-specialist-cores` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `beastbrain`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `rgs`, `moecot` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The architecture needs to allocate cognition across specialists rather than force one system to do every task.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Monolithic scaling and static tool lists do not provide local memories, bounded authority, specialist lifecycle, or readiness-aware routing.

## Core claim

[routing-heads-and-specialist-cores.core, support: argument] ASI scales through a lightweight routing head that selects bounded specialist cores with local tools, memory, and authority.

## Mechanism

- Register specialists with capabilities, costs, readiness, authority, and memories.
- Route tasks to the smallest adequate specialist.
- Fallback or escalate when confidence and readiness are insufficient.

## Interfaces

- Planning requests capabilities.
- Governance bounds specialists.
- Evidence updates readiness.

## Invariants

- Specialist authority is local.
- Routing decisions are logged.
- Fallback paths remain available.

## Failure modes

- Wrong specialist selection.
- Router overconfidence.
- Specialist authority leak.

## Minimal implementation

A router registry with capability metadata, cost, authority, and fallback rules.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Specialist routing accuracy test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Authority-bounded routing test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Fallback route test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `octopus_router` | Octopus Router Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Lightweight head/router with dynamically loaded specialist arms and local boundaries. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `beastbrain` | BeastBrain Cognitive Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Architecture lineage. Organism paradigm, SSD-native/geometrically verified intelligence. |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. |
| `rgs` | Ratcheting Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Bridge between active compression, procedural memory, benchmark frontiers, verified AI growth. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
